skill.name: caveman
skill.description: Compresses AI replies into terse technical wording while preserving exact technical meaning. Includes lite, full, ultra, and Wenyan-style intensity modes.
skill.summary: Makes technical replies shorter while keeping accuracy.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and prose matches as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation. Low risk remains because the skill asks for persistent response-style control, although it includes clarity exceptions.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks appear in command-like documentation, sentence patterns, technical examples, and a quoted SQL warning. They are Markdown text only, not Ruby backtick execution or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a Markdown instruction file with no Ruby, shell script, or executable handler. The flagged lines are examples and inline formatting, so the execution pattern does not apply.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are frontmatter prose and a destructive-operation example header. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, key handling, or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only natural-language instructions and examples. The suspicious matches occur in ordinary words, not in cryptographic APIs or algorithm selections.

low_findings.title: Persistent Style Control May Reduce Clarity
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to keep the terse style active across responses. This is not malicious, but broad persistence can reduce clarity if users forget the mode is active.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The persistence behavior is explicit and can affect later responses. The risk is limited because the skill also defines clarity exceptions for warnings and complex sequences.

content.user_title: Compress Technical Replies With Caveman Mode
content.value_statement: Long AI replies can waste context and slow technical work. This skill converts responses into short, direct wording while keeping technical details accurate.
actual_capabilities: Switches between lite, full, ultra, and Wenyan-style brevity modes.
Removes filler, hedging, pleasantries, and unnecessary articles from responses.
Keeps technical terms, quoted errors, and code blocks unchanged.
Uses terse patterns for explanations, causes, and next steps.
Pauses the terse style for security warnings and irreversible action confirmations.
limitations: Does not add new tools, APIs, or execution capabilities.
May sound informal in settings that require polished prose.
Can reduce readability for users who prefer complete sentences.
Wenyan modes require readers who understand classical Chinese style.

use_cases.title: Short Developer Debugging
use_cases.description: Get compact root-cause notes, likely fixes, and next steps during coding sessions.

use_cases.title: Context-Saving Reviews
use_cases.description: Keep code review feedback brief while preserving important technical details.

use_cases.title: Fast Terminal Assistance
use_cases.description: Receive direct command explanations and workflow guidance without long introductions.

prompt_templates.title: Enable Full Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman full mode for the rest of this conversation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for the default terse style.

prompt_templates.title: Use Lite Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman lite mode. Keep complete sentences, but remove filler.
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional setting that still needs concise replies.

prompt_templates.title: Compress a Debug Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain why this service is failing in caveman ultra mode. Keep exact error names unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging with strong token pressure.

prompt_templates.title: Switch Intensity During Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change in caveman full mode. Use normal mode only for security warnings.

output_examples.input: Why is my React component rendering again when props look the same?
output_examples.output: New object reference each render. Memo check fails. Move object creation outside render or memoize it.

output_examples.input: Explain database connection pooling in caveman lite mode.
output_examples.output: Connection pooling reuses open database connections. It avoids repeated connection setup and improves performance under load.

output_examples.input: Warn me before a destructive database operation.
output_examples.output: Warning: This operation permanently deletes data and cannot be undone. Verify backups and confirm the target environment first.

best_practices: Use lite mode for professional communication with non-technical stakeholders.
Use ultra mode only when context space is more important than natural wording.
Return to normal mode for sensitive decisions, legal text, or detailed explanations.
anti_patterns: Do not use terse mode when the user asks for a teaching explanation.
Do not compress warnings so much that risk or required confirmation becomes unclear.
Do not use Wenyan modes for teams that cannot read the style reliably.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It only changes response style through Markdown instructions.

faq.question: Can it reduce token usage?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to shorten replies by removing filler and compressing phrasing.

faq.question: Will code blocks change?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to leave code blocks unchanged.

faq.question: How do I stop the mode?
faq.answer: Ask for normal mode or say stop caveman.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security warnings?
faq.answer: It should use clearer normal wording for warnings and irreversible confirmations.

faq.question: Which tools support it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
